relations: The evolution of magic from Ancient Egypt's religious ceremonies to the mystical performances of the Middle Ages
Jean Eugène Robert-Houdin's art of magic was influenced by the foundational elements of magic that originated in Ancient Egypt
Harry Houdini's performances were part of the evolution of magic that has its roots in Ancient Egypt
David Copperfield's sophisticated illusions are part of the long history of magic that began in Ancient Egypt
Penn & Teller's innovative magic tricks are a continuation of the magical traditions that started in Ancient Egypt
Both Ancient Greece and Ancient Rome contributed to the development of magic tricks with public performances
The transition from the mystical and occult magic of the Middle Ages to the scientifically influenced magic of the Renaissance
John Dee was a prominent figure in the resurgence of magic during the Renaissance, using science and math in his acts
Girolamo Cardano contributed to the Renaissance's interest in magic with his performances that included scientific and mathematical elements
Both magicians revolutionized the art of magic in the 19th century with their performances
Both magicians are known for their sophisticated and technologically advanced magic tricks in the 20th century
